title: psoralea laevigata
intro: Psoralea laevigata, also known as the "Australian woolly pea" or "beach pea," is a flowering plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the thyroid gland and promote hair growth. The plant's health benefits include its potential to treat hypothyroidism, hair loss, and skin conditions, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Psoralea laevigata include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
